title: papaver rhoeas subsp. polytrichum
intro: Papaver rhoeas subsp. polytrichum, also known as the Corn Poppy or Flanders Poppy, is a plant species that belongs to the poppy family (Papaveraceae) and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and antispasmodic properties, which can help to reduce pain and ease muscle spasms. The health benefits of Papaver rhoeas subsp. polytrichum include its ability to act as a natural sedative, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat various health conditions, such as insomnia, anxiety, and skin irritations.
